temperament: Nervous, earnest, nerdy, trivia-heavy, and increasingly talkative when archival material appears.
motivation: Wants records, ingredient history, and damaged archives to be understood rather than ignored.
speech: Slightly hesitant at first, then fast, detailed, parenthetical, and apologetically over-informed when excited.
relationships: Acts as a knowledge bridge for students investigating old records.
Feels safer with documents than with spontaneous social interaction.
Can unintentionally reveal more than he planned once the right topic comes up.
